heading: Art. 114. Endangerment offenses
label: 914 Art. 114. Endangerment offenses
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)91450:708.(Art. 114), .May 5, 1950, ch. 169, § 164 Stat. 139

Amendments2016— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Any person subject to this chapter who fights or promotes, or is concerned in or connives at fighting a duel, or who, having knowledge of a challenge sent or about to be sent, fails to report the facts promptly to the proper authority, shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.”Pub. L. 114–328

children.text: is wrongful and reckless or is wanton; and
is likely to produce death or grievous bodily harm to another person;
who fights or promotes, or is concerned in or connives at fighting, a duel; or
who, having knowledge of a challenge sent or about to be sent, fails to report the facts promptly to the proper authority;

children.heading: .—Firearm Discharge, Endangering Human Life
children.label: c .—Firearm Discharge, Endangering Human Life
children.text: Any person subject to this chapter who, willfully and wrongly, discharges a firearm, under circumstances such as to endanger human life shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.

children.heading: .—Carrying Concealed Weapon
children.label: d .—Carrying Concealed Weapon
children.text: Any person subject to this chapter who unlawfully carries a dangerous weapon concealed on or about his person shall be punished as a court-martial may direct.
